landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, or ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation effective July 1, 2021. To note the complex substantive change request includes the sale of the assets of Wesley College to Delaware State University. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction. To require submission of any written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and the Delaware Higher Education Office. To direct a complex substantive change site visit as soon as practicable but no later than six months after July 1, 2021, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note that the following additional location is included within the institution's scope of accreditation: 120 North State Street, Dover, DE 19901. To direct a substantive change site visit as soon as practicable but no later than six months after the commencement of instruction at the additional location. To require immediate notification when instruction commences at the additional location. To note that the Commission may rescind this action if instruction does not commence within one calendar year from the date of this action. To request that the next self-study, in preparation for a visit in 2021-2022, provide further evidence of (1) periodic assessment of the effectiveness of all institutional policies and procedures and the manner in which they are implemented (Standard II); (2) accurately described academic programs and outcomes that enable students to understand and follow program requirements and time to completion (Standard III); (3) a general education program that is sufficient in scope and addresses the identified areas (Standard III); (4) clearly stated policies, processes and programs to admit, retain and facilitate the success of all students (Standard IV); (5) periodic assessment of the effectiveness of assessment processes utilized by the institution for the improvement of educational effectiveness (Standard V); (6) comprehensive planning for facilities, infrastructure and technology that include consideration of sustainability and deferred maintenance (Standard VI); (7) periodic assessment of the effectiveness of planning, resource allocation, institutional renewal, and availability of resources (Standard VI); and (8) documentation of the full implementation of the teach-out plan, including evidence that (a) required notifications have taken place, (b) student records have been transferred to Delaware State University, and (c) a final version of the integration planning team milestones including timeframe and status (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action.
landing_actions.action_label_short: Change of Legal Status (effective July 1, 2021)

163578.institution_unique_name: Notre Dame of Maryland University | Baltimore | Maryland
163578.latest_action_label: Merger of Maryland University of Integrative Health with Notre Dame of Maryland University (effective February 1, 2024)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective February 1, 2024. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Maryland University of Integrative Health with Notre Dame of Maryland University, effective, February 1, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction by email to [email protected] or, if there is a subsequent delay in the anticipated date of the closing of the transaction, to require immediate notification to the Commission of the revised anticipated transaction date. To note that Notre Dame of Maryland University is the surviving institution. To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the integrated institution. To note the Maryland University of Integrative Health will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and effectuating the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note the action for Maryland University of Integrative Health on January 25, 2024. To note that the main campus of Maryland University of Integrative Health at 7750 Montpelier Road, Laurel, MD 20723 will become an additional location included within the scope of accreditation of Notre Dame of Maryland University. To require immediate notification when 7750 Montpelier Road, Laurel, MD 20723 becomes an additional location of Notre Dame of Maryland University by email to [email protected]. To note that the Commission may rescind this action if instruction does not commence within one calendar year from the date of this action. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due July 1, 2024, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all outstanding required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and the Maryland Higher Education Commission (Standard II); and, (3) written evidence of the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations including post-transaction requirements. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus at 4701 N. Charles Street, Baltimore, MD 21210 and the additional location at 7750 Montpelier Road, Laurel, MD 20723 as soon as practicable but no later than six months after the anticipated date of the transaction, February 1, 2024. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any development reveals additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h). To note that the institution is currently in self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2025-2026.

185129.institution_unique_name: New Jersey City University | Jersey City | New Jersey
185129.latest_action_label: Merger of New Jersey City University with Kean University (effective July 1, 2026)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective July 1, 2026, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of New Jersey City University with Kean University, effective July 1, 2026, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note that the accreditation cease date will be determined after the United States Department of Education (USDE) has approved the merger application. To note that Kean University is the surviving institution. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To note New Jersey City University will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and to effectuate the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note that written documentation of review or approval from the following agency has been received: the New Jersey Office of the Secretary of Higher Education (OSHE) and the New Jersey State Legislature. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due August 17, 2026 providing: (1) the updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) an update on the date New Jersey City University's degree granting authority will cease (Requirement of Affiliation 1); and (3) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval from all required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including but not limited to: the United States Department of Education (Standard II). To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action. To note the Commission action for Kean University on April 23, 2026.

185262.latest_action_label: Merger of New Jersey City University with Kean University (effective July 1, 2026)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, or ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation effective July 1, 2026, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of New Jersey City University with Kean University, effective July 1, 2026, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note that Kean University is the surviving institution. To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the integrated institution. To note that New Jersey City University will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and effectuating the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note that the accreditation cease date will be determined after the United States Department of Education (USDE) has approved the merger application. To note that the main campus of New Jersey City University at 2039 Kennedy Boulevard, Jersey City, NJ 07305 will become an additional location included within the scope of accreditation of Kean University. To require immediate notification when New Jersey City University becomes an additional location of Kean University by email to [email protected]. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay in the anticipated date of the closing of the transaction by email to [email protected]. To note that written documentation of review or approval from the following agencies has been received: the New Jersey Office of the Secretary of Higher Education (OSHE) and the New Jersey State Legislature. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due September 1, 2026, documenting (1) updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education (Standard II); (3) evidence of formal conflict-of-interest policies and recusal procedures post-merger transition (Standard II); (4) evidence of individualized credit evaluations and teach-out communication provided to New Jersey City University students (Standard II); (5) sufficient learning experiences and resources to support both the institution's programs of study and the academic progress of all student populations (Standard III); (6) evidence of the integration of New Jersey City University athletic, student life, and other extracurricular activities that are regulated by the same academic, fiscal, and administrative principles and procedures that govern all other programs (Standard IV); (7) evidence of the integration of the New Jersey City University Foundation and its disbursement of scholarship funds (Standard IV); (8) assessment of student learning and achievement that demonstrates that students have accomplished educational goals consistent with their programs of study (Standard V); (9) evidence of migration of New Jersey City University data into the Student Success Data Collaborative (Standard V); (10) evidence that its student learning programs are characterized by coherence and appropriate assessment of student achievement throughout its educational offerings, at both institutional and program levels, with evidence that faculty and appropriate institutional stakeholders have considered and used assessment results to improve teaching and learning (Standard V); (11) the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location (Standard VI); and (12) evidence of the implementation of a unified governance structure (Standard VII) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standard Guidelines). To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the additional location at 2039 Kennedy Boulevard Jersey City, NJ 07305, and to the main campus at 1000 Morris Avenue, Union, NJ 07083, as soon as practicable but no later than six months after July 1, 2026 the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision, or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR 602.22(h). The next evaluation visit is scheduled for 2029-2030. To note the Commission action for New Jersey City University on April 23, 2026.

185590.institution_unique_name: Montclair State University | Montclair | New Jersey
185590.latest_action_label: Merger of Bloomfield College with Montclair State University (effective June 30, 2023)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, or ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation effective June 30, 2023. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Bloomfield College with Montclair State University, effective June 30, 2023, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note that Montclair State University is the surviving institution and Bloomfield College will become an additional location of Montclair State University. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay in the anticipated date of the closing of the transaction by email to [email protected]. To request a supplemental information report, due August 15, 2023, documenting (1) updated status of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and the New Jersey Office of the Secretary of Higher Education (Standard II and former Requirement of Affiliation 5); (3) assessment of student learning and achievement that demonstrates that students have accomplished educational goals consistent with their programs of study (Standard V and Requirements of Affiliation 8 and 9); (4) evidence that its student learning programs are characterized by coherence and appropriate assessment of student achievement throughout its educational offerings, at both institutional and program levels, with evidence that faculty and appropriate institutional stakeholders have considered and used assessment results to improve teaching and learning (Standard V and Requirement of Affiliation 9); (5) sufficient support to sustain the assessment of student achievement and to communicate results of assessment to stakeholders (Standard V); (6) periodic assessment of the effectiveness of assessment processes utilized by the institution for the improvement of educational effectiveness (Standard V); (7) sufficiency of resources to fulfill its mission and goals to support its educational purposes and programs (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 11); and (8) clearly stated, aligned and coherent institutional and unit objectives, with evidence of the implementation of a systematic, organized, and sustained assessment process demonstrating that institutional and unit stakeholders have considered and used assessment results in planning, budgeting, and the improvement of institutional and unit effectiveness (Standard VI and former Requirement of Affiliation 10). To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the merged institutions. To note that the main campus of Bloomfield College at 467 Franklin Street, Bloomfield, NJ 07003 will become an additional location included within the scope of accreditation of Montclair State University. To require immediate notification when Bloomfield College becomes an additional location of Montclair State University by email to [email protected]. To direct a Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus at Montclair State University and the newly acquired additional location at 467 Franklin Street, Bloomfield, NJ 07003 as soon as practicable but no later than six months after June 30, 2023, the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h). To note that the institution is currently in self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2025-2026. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action.

192271.institution_unique_name: LIM College | New York | New York

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective December 1, 2025. To note the complex substantive change includes the sale of the institution to JEF New York, Inc. (JEFNY), a subsidiary of Japan Educational Foundation (JEF), effective December 1, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the anticipated closing date, by email to [email protected]. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due February 27, 2026, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change; (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all outstanding required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and New York State Department of Education (Standard II); and (3) documentation of any new agreements or related entity disclosures for collaborations with JEFNY affiliated institutions (Related Entities Policy and Procedures). To note that accreditation status cannot be acquired by, transferred, or extended to any third party (Related Entities Policy and Procedures). To direct the evaluation team, scheduled to visit in spring 2026, to review the institution's establishment of and compliance with a written conflict of interest policy designed to ensure the impartiality of the governing body, including documentation of regular or systematic disclosures or reporting of conflicts of interest and/or recusals (Standard VII). To direct a complex substantive change site visit to take place as soon as practicable but no later than six months after December 1, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To direct the evaluation team to verify the information in the complex substantive change during the on-site evaluation visit. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations including post-transaction requirements. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision, or if the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR 602.22(h). A new comprehensive evaluation is not required because the institution is currently in self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2025-2026.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, or ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective June 30, 2025. To note the complex substantive change request includes Northeastern University (Northeastern New York City) becoming the sole member of Marymount Manhattan College (MMC) and subsequently merging with MMC. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction by email to [email protected], or, if there is a subsequent delay in the anticipated date of the closing, to require immediate notification to the Commission of the revised anticipated transaction date. To further note that accreditation as approved by the Middle States Commission on Higher Education (MSCHE) will cease on March 31, 2026. To note MMC will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and to effectuate the merger process with the New York State Education Department (NYSED) and United States Department of Education (USDE). To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report due October 1, 2025, providing: (1) the updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Policy and Procedures) and (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval from all required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including but not limited to the NYSED and USDE (Standard II and Substantive Change Policy and Procedures). To note MMC will cease with MSCHE following the merger. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to main campus as soon as practicable but no later than six months after June 30, 2025. To note Northeastern University will become a related entity to MMC. To remind the institution and any related entities of their responsibility for disclosing, documenting, and explaining all significant matters and relationships that may impact compliance, including but not limited to governance, independence, autonomy, management, administration, and financial viability (Related Entities Policy and Procedures). To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or if the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission immediately about any and all significant developments that may impact the institution's closure (Standard VII).

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective June 1, 2026. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Albany College of Pharmacy and Health Sciences with Russell Sage College, effective June 1, 2026, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To note that Russell Sage College is the surviving institution. To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the integrated institution. To note that Albany College of Pharmacy and Health Sciences will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and effectuating the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note that Albany College of Pharmacy and Health Sciences' accreditation cease date will be determined after United States Department of Education has approved the merger application. To include the following additional location within the institution's scope of accreditation: 106 New Scotland Avenue, Albany, NY 12208. To require immediate notification in the institution portal when instruction commences at the additional location. To request a supplemental information report, due July 31, 2026, providing (1) updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including but not limited to the United States Department of Education and the New York State Education Department (Standard II); and (3) written evidence of the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus of 65 1st Street, Troy, NY 12180, and to the additional location at 106 New Scotland Avenue, Albany, NY 12208, as soon as practicable but no later than six months after June 1, 2026, the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR 602.22(h). To note that the institution will begin self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2028-2029. To note the action for Albany College of Pharmacy and Health Sciences on February 26, 2026.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the supplemental information report directed by the Commission action of June 27, 2024. To accept the institution's request to voluntarily surrender its accreditation and terminate its membership effective June 2, 2025. To note that a teach-out plan and teach-out agreements are not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the New York State Department of Education and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education. To note that the United States Department of Education Office of Federal Student Aid determined reasonable cause for the change in accreditor and issued approval for the change in accreditor on November 8, 2024, pursuant to DCL ID GEN 25-03 dated May 1, 2025. To note receipt of written notification, dated June 2, 2025, that the United States Department of Education acknowledges the new accrediting agency as the institution's primary accrediting agency. To note further that the Commission has determined the institutional change of accrediting agency is voluntary pursuant to 34 C.F.R. 602.14(a). To note that accreditation ceased on June 30, 2025. As of July 1, 2025, Sarah Lawrence College is no longer accredited by the Middle States Commission on Higher Education. To note the New England Commission of Higher Education is the institutional accreditor for the institution and will now serve as the designated Title IV gatekeeper for the institution. To note that student records will be maintained at Sarah Lawrence College, One Meadway, Bronxville, NY 10708.

To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus at 3 Times Square, New York, NY 10036 and additional location at 53 East 124th Street as soon as practicable but no later than six months after the anticipated date of the transaction, December 31, 2024. landing_actions.action_label: Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to acknowledge receipt of the notification, dated March 2, 2026, of the institution's intent to change their primary accreditor, voluntarily surrender accreditation, and terminate membership. landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective September 1, 2025. To note that the complex substantive change request includes the sale of the School of Visual Arts (SVA) to the SVA Alumni Society (SVAAS). To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To require immediate notification by email to [email protected] if there are any material changes to the information provided in the substantive change request prior to the closing of the transaction. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due November 3 , 2025, providing, (1) an updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review or approval of the transaction from all required licensing, regulatory, or legal entities, including but not limited to: the United States Department of Education, the New York State Education Department Board of Regents, the Charities Bureau of the Office of the New York State Attorney General, the Attorney General of the State of New York, the New York State Approving Agency for the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs, the New York Higher Education Services Corporation, and U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Student and Visitor Exchange Program (Standard II); (3) material updates to corporate governance or board structure, including but not limited to submission of an updated governance organizational chart and any applicable conflict-of-interest policies (Standard VII) ; (4) written evidence of the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting any applicable changes; (5) documentation demonstrating how the institution ensures the design, delivery, and assessment of student learning experiences are consistent with institutional expectations for academic quality, in alignment with (Standard III and Standard V); and (6) documentation of financial planning and resource allocation that demonstrates the institution has sufficient resources to sustain operations post-transaction (Standard VI). To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus of the School of Visual Arts in New York, NY, as soon as practicable but no later than six months after September 1, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction, to assess the institution's compliance with Commission standards in governance, operations, academic quality, and student support services. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision or if the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. A new comprehensive visit is not required because the institution is currently in self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2025-2026.

To note the complex substantive change request includes the member substitution of Ursuline College and the merger of Ursuline College with Gannon University. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To note that Gannon University is the surviving institution. To note that the main campus of Ursuline College at 2550 Lander Road, Pepper Pike, OH 44124 will become an additional location included within the scope of accreditation of Gannon University. To require immediate notification when Ursuline College becomes an additional location of Gannon University by email to [email protected]. To note that written documentation of review or approval has been received from the Pennsylvania Department of Education. To request a supplemental information report, due September 30, 2025, providing (1) updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all required licensing, regulatory, or legal entities, including the United States Department of Education, the Ohio Department of Higher Education and Workforce and the Ohio Attorney General, if applicable (Standard II); and (3) the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the additional location at 2550 Lander Road, Pepper Pike, OH 44124, and to the main campus at 109 University Square, Erie, PA, as soon as practicable but no later than six months after June 30, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision, or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR 602.22(h). To note that the institution will begin self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2028-2029.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective May 30, 2025. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Keystone College into Keystone College, LLC, with Washington Institute for Education and Research (WIER) as the sole member, effective May 30, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction by email to [email protected], or, if there is a subsequent delay in the anticipated date of the closing of the transaction, to require immediate notification to the Commission of the revised anticipated transaction date. To note the institution will maintain its accreditation following the transaction. To note that written evidence of required approvals has been provided by the Pennsylvania Department of Education. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due September 2, 2025, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change; (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all outstanding required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including but not limited to the Pennsylvania Office of Attorney General and the United States Department of Education(Standard II); (3) evidence of a comprehensive financial planning process, multi-year budget, annual independent audit verifying financial viability and internal controls, and documented funding base adequate to support the educational purposes and ensure financial stability that includes a financial development plan and resources from any related entities (Standard VI); (4) planning that integrates goals for institutional effectiveness and improvement, including overall institutional improvement and the results of institutional assessments as related to the complex substantive change transaction and efforts to expand international recruitment (Standard VI); (5) an update on the status of recruiting and hiring a qualified Chief Financial Officer (Standard VI); (6) evidence of the status of the institution's membership in the National Council for State Authorization Reciprocity Agreements and compliance with all membership requirements (Standard VI); (7) evidence of the implementation of a written conflict of interest policy designed to ensure the impartiality of the governing body (Standard VII); and (8) a clearly articulated and transparent governance structure that outlines roles, responsibilities, and accountability for inclusive decision making by each constituency, including the institution's legally constituted governing body and any related entities, including any updates to Keystone College Guide to Governance and Policy (Standard VII) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standard Guidelines). To direct a complex substantive change site visit as soon as practicable but no later than six months after May 30, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note the complex substantive change site visit will occur at the same time as the show cause visit. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations including post-transaction requirements. To note the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution and the related entity of their responsibility for disclosing, documenting, and explaining all significant matters and relationships involving related entities that may impact its compliance including but not limited to governance, independence and autonomy, management and administration, and financial viability and stability (Related Entities Policy and Procedures). To note the Commission's action of February 27, 2025, requiring the institution to show cause why its accreditation should not be withdrawn. The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation.

214883.latest_action_label: Merger of Lackawanna College with Peirce College (effective June 30, 2025)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective June 30, 2025. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Lackawanna College with Peirce College, effective, June 30, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email [email protected]. To note that Lackawanna College is the surviving institution. To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the integrated institution. To note that Peirce College will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and effectuating the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note that written documentation of review or approval from the following agency has been received: Pennsylvania Attorney General. To request a supplemental information report, due October 1, 2025: (1) the updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) an update on the date Peirce College's degree granting authority will cease (Requirement of Affiliation 1); and (3) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval from all required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including but not limited to: the United States Department of Education and the Pennsylvania Department of Education (Standard II). To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action. To note the action for Lackawanna College on April 24, 2025.

To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action. To note the action for Lackawanna College on April 24, 2025.
landing_actions.source_title: MSCHE Statement of Accreditation Status - Peirce College

landing_actions.action_label: Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to acknowledge receipt of the notification, dated April 2, 2026, of the institution's intent to change their primary accreditor, voluntarily surrender accreditation, and terminate membership. To accept the institution's request to voluntarily surrender its accreditation and intent to terminate its membership effective May 31, 2026. To note that the United States Department of Education Office of Federal Student Aid determined reasonable cause for the change in accreditor and issued approval for the change in accreditor on November 26, 2025, pursuant to DCL ID GEN 25-03 dated May 1, 2025. To note further that the Commission has determined the institutional change of accrediting agency is voluntary pursuant to 34 C.F.R. 602.14(a). To note that a teach-out plan and teach-out agreements are not necessary because the institution will retain its degree-granting authority from the Pennsylvania Department of Education and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education. To note the accreditation cease date will be determined upon receipt of written verification from the New England Commission on Higher Education of the effective date for change in accreditors.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the supplemental information report directed by Commission action of June 22, 2023. To reject the supplemental information report because the quality and substance of the report were insufficient to permit Commission review. To note that the institution violated the conditions set by the Commission action of June 22, 2023. To note that the institution failed to submit the required substantive change request for institutional closure, as directed by Commission action of July 27, 2023. To note that the institution has failed to meet its membership responsibilities (Dues and Fees Policy and Procedures and Bylaws 4.04). To require the substantive change request for institutional closure due September 6, 2023. To note that accreditation for the institution and all of its locations will cease on August 31, 2023. To acknowledge receipt of the notification dated August 16, 2023, of the institution's request to voluntarily surrender accreditation and terminate membership. To reject the institution's request to voluntarily surrender its accreditation and membership.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the updated teach-out plan. To approve the teach-out agreements with: (1) Asbury Theological Seminary, Wilmore, KY; (2) Borough of Manhattan Community College, New York, NY; (3) Eastern University, St. Davids, PA; (4) Fordham University, Bronx, NY; (5) Gordon-Conwell Theological Seminary, Hamilton, MA; (6) Helene Fuld College of Nursing, New York, NY; (7) Houghton University, Houghton, NY; (8) Kairos University, Sioux Falls, SD; (9) Mercy College, Dobbs Ferry, NY; (10) Messiah University, Mechanicsburg, PA; (11) New Brunswick Theological Seminary, New Brunswick, NJ; (12) Northeastern Seminary at Roberts Wesleyan College, Rochester, NY; (13) Pillar College, Newark, NJ; and (14) Roberts Wesleyan University, Rochester, NY. To postpone a decision on the teach-out agreement with Seminario Evangelico de Puerto Rico, San Juan, PR. To request an updated teach-out plan due August 15, 2023, to include (1) evidence that communications regarding student records has occurred; (2) evidence that financial aid counseling has been conducted; (3) evidence that onsite transfer events have taken place; (4) copies of any new signed teach-out agreements, including any documentation supporting the agreements; and (5) evidence of external approvals, including the New York State Education Department and the United States Department of Education (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). To note that Houghton University, Houghton, NY has been designated as the final repository of all Alliance University student records. To note that students may obtain academic records at Houghton University, One Willard Ave. P.O. Box 128, Houghton, NY 14744. In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation, and include any signed, teach-out agreements that the institution has entered into or intends to enter into with another institution. To note the Commission will reject the teach-out plan and/or agreements and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided.

accred-art-institute-of-pittsburgh-the--.latest_action_label: To note the teach-out plan with teach-out agreements, due April 1, 2019, was not received.

landing_actions.action_label: To note the teach-out plan with teach-out agreements, due April 1, 2019, was not received. To note the federal equity receiver, custodian, and liquidator, after repeated requests, has failed to confirm the status of The Art Institute of Pittsburgh and failed to provide a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan with teach-out agreements. To require a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan with teach-out agreements, due May 3, 2019, from the federal equity receiver, custodian, and liquidator documenting evidence of: (1) communications and information provided by the Receiver to the Pennsylvania Department of Education and the United States Department of Education relating to AI Pittsburgh relating to closure; (2) an updated teach-out plan with copies of all teach-out agreements relating to The Art Institute of Pittsburgh, including, but not limited to, verification that teach-out agreements received on January 25, 2019, from the institution are verified, ratified, or otherwise authorized by the Receiver; (3) the status of the term that abruptly ended on March 8, 2019, including whether grades were issued and the number of degrees that were awarded; (4) provisions for student records, including how students can obtain transcripts and the appropriate arrangement for the permanent disposition of all student records held by the institution so that students and alumni will be able to obtain accurate and complete transcripts in the future; (5) notification to all students that the institution's accreditation ceased effective March 13, 2019; and (6) public disclosure regarding the institution's accreditation status provided wherever the institution's web pages, publications, and announcements make reference to MSCHE accreditation and in all other appropriate places and venues.

landing_actions.action_label: To reject the teach-out plan because it did not meet the criteria established in the Teach-Out Plans and Agreement(s) Policy and Procedures. To approve the teach-out agreement with Florida Memorial University for the following programs: Business Administration (BS), Finance (BS), Criminal Justice (BA), Psychology (BS), and Healthcare with a concentration in Administration (BS). To reject the teach-out agreement with United International College, Miramar, FL because ASA College was unable to provide evidence of an institutional accreditor for the institution that is recognized by the United Stated Department of Education. To note that the teach-out agreements with Keiser University, Fort Lauderdale, FL, and Southeastern University, Lakeland, FL, are rejected as they have been rescinded by the institutions. To note that the Commission is attempting to verify where records can be obtained. To note that the institution has failed to submit evidence of the following despite repeated requests by the Commission, including (1) communications with all constituents about plans for closure; (2) financial aid counseling provided to students; (3) of the release of transcripts (or the production and issuance of transcripts for any students wishing to transfer to another institution); (4) communications regarding student records has occurred; (5) a complete list of students currently enrolled in each program at all locations of the institution, their individual status, and the program requirements each student has completed; (6) information regarding all students, including online and/or international students and students in Florida and New York, in all ASA College programs and how those students will be accommodated through teach-out agreements; (7) availability of ASA College staff to assist students as part of the teach-out plan; (8) appropriate information and public disclosures posted at ASA College websites, including those for its New York and Florida locations; (9) documentation of appropriate advisement and support services specific to the unique needs of international students; (10) responsiveness to NJCAA reporting requirements; (11) payment of contracts for disposition of student, employee, and financial records; (12) the status of the institution's payroll obligations; (13) provisions to assist faculty and staff to secure employment; (14) compliance with reporting requirements from local, state, and federal authorities; and (15) response to formal complaint regarding nonpayment of contractual obligations with athletics officials in Florida. To note the Commission will work with the United States Department of Education (USDE) and the appropriate State agencies, to the extent feasible, to assist students in finding reasonable opportunities to complete their education without additional charge in accordance with federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.24(d) as a result of the institution's closure and its repeated failures to provide requested information. To remind the institution of the Commission's action of January 31, 2023, to require the submission of a substantive change request for institutional closure. To remind the institution of the Commission's action of February 8, 2023, noting that the institution failed to submit the substantive change request for institutional closure as directed by the Commission. To note that accreditation will cease on March 1, 2023.

landing_actions.action_label: To rescind the Commission's action of October 28, 2021 to warn the institution, because of additional information that would have affected the Commission's decision, including continued turnover of the governing board and related information from state agencies.To place the institution on probation in lieu of the warning and note that the institution's accreditation is in jeopardy because of insufficient evidence that the institution is currently in compliance with Standard VI (Planning Resources, and Institutional Improvement), Standard VII (Governance, Leadership, and Administration), and Requirements of Affiliation 11, 12, 13 and 14. To note that the institution remains accredited while on probation. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which an institution may be in non-compliance, which started October 28, 2021. To reject the monitoring report because the report provided limited and evasive responses to requested information, lacked full disclosure and transparency, and did not present evidence and analysis in a manner conducive to Commission review. To request that the institution resubmit the monitoring report, due January 3, 2022, demonstrating evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with 1. documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development (Requirement of Affiliation 11); 2. demonstrated sufficiency of resources to fulfill its mission and goals and to support its educational purposes and programs (Standard VI); 3. clearly defined decision-making processes, with clear assignment of responsibility and accountability (Standard VI); 4. strategies to assess the adequacy and efficient utilization of institutional resources required to support the institution's mission and goals (Standard VI); 5. a clearly articulated and transparent governance structure that outlines roles, responsibilities, and accountability for decision-making by each constituency (Standard VII and Requirements of Affiliation 12 and 13); 6. a governing body that has sufficient independence and expertise to ensure the integrity of the institution (Standard VII); 7. a governing body that is informed in all its operations by principles of good practice in board governance (Standard VII); 8. a governance structure that allows it to realize its stated mission and goals in a way that benefits the institution, its students and the constituencies it serves (Standard VII and Requirements of Affiliation 12 and 13); 9. adherence to its policies and other governing documents, including but not limited to Bylaws, to legally constitute its governing body and change the Board composition (Standards II and VII and Requirement of Affiliation 13); and 10. periodic assessment of the effectiveness of governance, leadership, and administration (Standard VII). To request that the monitoring report also include 1. demonstrated adherence to all issues of ethics and integrity as outlined in the standard (Standard II); 2. evidence of compliance with all applicable government laws and regulations, including regulations for all states where the institution operates (Requirement of Affiliation 5); and 3. evidence and documentation relating to the investigation of the New York City Commission on Human Rights and related media reports (Standard II). To remind the institution and its governing body of its obligation to inform the Commission immediately about any and all significant developments relevant to this action and to make freely available to the Commission accurate, fair, and complete information on all aspects of the institution and its operations (Requirement of Affiliation 14). To direct a follow-up team visit following submission of the monitoring report. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. Upon reaffirmation of accreditation, the next evaluation visit is scheduled for 2023-2024. To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by January 3, 2022 a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation, and include any teach-out agreements that the institution has entered into or intends to enter into with another institution.

landing_actions.action_label: Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to acknowledge receipt of the supplemental information report requested by the Commission action of November 4, 2024. To accept the institution's request to voluntarily surrender its accreditation and terminate its membership effective March 31, 2025. As of April 1, 2025, Athabasca University is no longer accredited by the Middle States Commission on Higher Education. To note that the institution does not participate in Title IV/student financial aid programs. To note that a teach-out plan is not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the Government of Alberta's Ministry of Advanced Education.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the notification, dated October 23, 2024, of the institution's intent to voluntarily surrender accreditation and terminate membership. Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to request a supplemental information report, due December 13, 2024, providing: (1) evidence that state or federal requirements and approvals regarding the surrender of the institution's accreditation status have been obtained or are in progress and that the institution has ascertained and verified that any state and federal requirements, including requirements from the Government of Alberta's Ministry of Advanced Education, related to surrender or loss of accreditation have been met (Standard II); (2) confirmation that the institution does not participate in title IV/student financial aid programs (Standard II); (3) copies of communications to the institution's community, including all board members, students, faculty, staff, and others with significant roles, related to the surrender of accreditation as appropriate (Standard II and Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures); and (4) the anticipated date of the voluntary surrender (MARCHE Bylaws, Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures, and Accreditation Review Cycle and Monitoring Policy and Procedures). To note that a teach-out plan is not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the Government of Alberta's Ministry of Advanced Education.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the teach-out plan and teach-out agreement. To approve the teach-out agreement with Montclair State University, Montclair, NY and to note that student records, programs, and services will transfer from Bloomfield College to Montclair State University. The purpose of the teach-out is to provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to a new institution as a result of the transaction. To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, due August 15, 2023, an updated teach-out plan that must include: (1) signed teach-out agreements, including any documentation supporting the agreements, to cover all academic programs; (2) evidence that required notifications to all relevant stakeholders have taken place; (3) evidence that the institution has notified students of any additional charges; (4) evidence that communications regarding student records has occurred; and (5) evidence that financial aid counseling has been conducted (Teach-Out Plan and Agreements Policy and Procedures). To note that Bloomfield College's accreditation will cease on June 30, 2024. To note that Montclair State University, Upper Montclair, NJ has been designated as the repository of all Bloomfield College student records. To require notification when student records have been transferred to Montclair State University by email to [email protected]. To note the action for Montclair State University on June 22, 2023.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, or ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation effective June 30, 2023. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Bloomfield College with Montclair State University, effective June 30, 2023, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To note that Montclair State University is the surviving institution. To further note that Bloomfield College's accreditation will cease effective June 30, 2024, based on the Commission's grant of a waiver of the Teach-Out Plan and Agreement(s) Policy and Procedure Section III.E.4.b. for up to one year following the date of the transaction in order to implement an approved teach-out plan, and to align with the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note Bloomfield College will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements. To request a supplemental information report, due August 15, 2023, providing (1) updated status of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) update on the date Bloomfield College's degree granting authority will cease (Requirement of Affiliation 1); (3) update on the status of proposed New Jersey Assembly Bill 5470 and its impact on the complex substantive change (former Requirement of Affiliation 5); and (4) written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and the New Jersey Office of the Secretary of Higher Education (Standard II; former Requirement of Affiliation 5). To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations including post-transaction requirements. To note the action for Montclair State University on June 22, 2023.

To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the supplemental information report. To acknowledge receipt of the institution's Intent to Voluntarily Surrender accreditation and terminate membership. To request a supplemental information report due June 1, 2020, providing: (1) evidence of New York State Department of Education requirements and all necessary approvals regarding the surrender of the institution's accreditation status; (2) evidence of the request made to the Association of Theological Schools (ATS) and the United States Department of Education (USDE) to transfer title IV gatekeeper status to ATS, including any relevant correspondence or notification from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office; (3) evidence of the approval of the transfer of title IV gatekeeper status to the Association of Theological Schools (ATS) by the United States Department of Education, including any relevant correspondence or notification from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office; and (4) evidence of notification to the institution's constituents related to the surrender of accreditation (Requirements of Affiliation 5 and 6, and Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). To further request that the institution complete and submit for approval, by June 1, 2020, a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education, upon voluntary surrender of accreditation, and include any teach-out agreements that the institution has entered into or intends to enter into with another institution. To note that accreditation will cease effective July 1, 2020.

landing_actions.action_label: To rescind the Commission's action of November 16, 2023, to place the institution on probation because of new information regarding the institution's financial and operational status received June 17, 2024. In lieu of probation, to require the institution to show cause, by August 1, 2024, to demonstrate why its accreditation should not be withdrawn because of insufficient evidence that the institution is in compliance with Standard II (Ethics and Integrity), Standard III (Design and Delivery of the Student Learning Experience), Standard IV (Support of the Student Experience), Standard V (Educational Effectiveness Assessment), Standard VI (Planning, Resources, and Institutional Improvement), Standard VII (Leadership, Governance, and Administration), and Requirement of Affiliation 2. To note that the institution remains accredited while on show cause. To note further that federal regulations limit the period during which the institution may be in non-compliance, which started on November 16, 2023. To require a show cause report, due August 1, 2024, documenting evidence that the institution has achieved and can sustain ongoing compliance with Commission standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable federal regulatory requirements. The show cause report must include evidence (1) that the institution is operational, with students actively enrolled in its degree programs (Requirement of Affiliation 2); (2) that the institution honors its contracts and commitments and adheres to its policies, including timely payroll obligations (Standard II); (3) of a core of faculty (full-or part-time) and/or other appropriate professionals with sufficient responsibility to the institution to assure the continuity and coherence of the institution's educational programs (Standard III); (4) that the institution commits to student retention, persistence, completion, and success through a coherent and effective support system, sustained by qualified professionals, which enhances the quality of the learning environment, contributes to the educational experience, and fosters student success (Standard IV); (5) of assessment of student achievement throughout its educational offerings, at both institutional and program levels, with evidence faculty and appropriate institutional stakeholders have considered and used assessment results to improve teaching and learning (Standard V); (6) of clearly stated, aligned, and coherent institutional and unit objectives, with evidence of the implementation of a systematic, organized, and sustained assessment process demonstrating that institutional and unit stakeholders have considered and used assessment results in planning, budgeting, and the improvement of institutional and unit effectiveness (Standard VI); (7) of documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development (Standard VI); (8) of adequate fiscal and human resources, including physical and technical infrastructure, to support operations (Standard VI); and (9) of qualified administrators, sufficient in number, to enable the Chief Executive Officer to discharge his/her duties effectively (Standard VII) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standard Guidelines). To request that the show cause report also include further evidence of (1) the periodic assessment of the effectiveness of all institutional policies and procedures and the manner in which they are implemented (Standard II) and (2) compliance with its program responsibilities under existing federal Title IV (Standard VI). To require that the institution complete and submit for approval, by August 1, 2024, an updated comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan and signed teach-out agreements with appropriate teach-out partner institutions, including any documentation supporting the agreements (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To direct an on-site show cause visit following submission of the report and the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements. The purpose of the on-site show cause visit is to verify the information provided in the show cause report and the institution's ongoing and sustainable compliance with Commission standards for accreditation, requirements of affiliation, policies and procedures, and applicable regulatory requirements. To direct a prompt Commission liaison visit to discuss Commission expectations. To note the institution will be invited to present before the Commission when it meets to consider the institution's show cause report. The date of the next evaluation will be determined upon reaffirmation of accreditation.

To request an updated teach-out plan, due September 1, 2019, with evidence of (1) signed copies of any updated teach-out agreements, including any documentation supporting the agreements; (2) the status of the implementation of the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, including evidence of continued resources adequate to support teach-out operations prior to institutional closure; (3) the issuance of transcripts for any students wishing to transfer to another institution; (4) notification of the Commission's action to The College of New Rochelle community, including all governing board members, students, faculty, and staff, and others with significant roles; (5) notification to current and prospective students that the institution's accreditation will cease effective January 1, 2020; and (6) public disclosure regarding the institution's accreditation status provided wherever the institution's web pages, publications, and announcements make reference to accreditation and in all other appropriate places and venues (Requirement of Affiliation 6: Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the institution's intent to voluntarily surrender candidate for accreditation status and terminate membership. Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to request a supplemental information report due September 1, 2023, providing: (1) the anticipated date of the voluntary surrender (MARCHE Bylaws and Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures); (2) state or federal requirements and written evidence of required approvals regarding the surrender of the institution's candidate for accreditation status and that the institution has ascertained and verified that any state or federal requirements related to surrender or loss of accreditation have been met (Standard II); (3) information regarding any implications of the surrender or loss of accreditation on Title IV/student financial aid program participation including any correspondence, notification, and/or written evidence of approvals from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office of the United States Department of Education, specifically verification that Western Association of Schools and Colleges - Senior College & University Commission (WSCUC) is the designated gatekeeper for USDE (Standard II); and (4) copies of communications to the institution's community (students and other constituents) related to the surrender or loss of candidate for accreditation status as appropriate (Standard II, Standard VII, Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures). To note that a teach-out plan is not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the New York State Education Department, and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education.

landing_actions.notes: To acknowledge receipt of the institution's intent to voluntarily surrender candidate for accreditation status and terminate membership. Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to request a supplemental information report due September 1, 2023, providing: (1) the anticipated date of the voluntary surrender (MARCHE Bylaws and Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures); (2) state or federal requirements and written evidence of required approvals regarding the surrender of the institution's candidate for accreditation status and that the institution has ascertained and verified that any state or federal requirements related to surrender or loss of accreditation have been met (Standard II); (3) information regarding any implications of the surrender or loss of accreditation on Title IV/student financial aid program participation including any correspondence, notification, and/or written evidence of approvals from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office of the United States Department of Education, specifically verification that Western Association of Schools and Colleges - Senior College & University Commission (WSCUC) is the designated gatekeeper for USDE (Standard II); and (4) copies of communications to the institution's community (students and other constituents) related to the surrender or loss of candidate for accreditation status as appropriate (Standard II, Standard VII, Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures). To note that a teach-out plan is not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the New York State Education Department, and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education.
landing_actions.source_title: MSCHE Statement of Accreditation Status - Elmezzi Graduate School of Molecular Medicine

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the institution's Intent to Voluntarily Surrender accreditation and terminate membership. To request a supplemental information report due June 1, 2020, providing: (1) evidence of Pennsylvania Department of Education requirements and all necessary approvals regarding the surrender of the institution's accreditation status; (2) evidence of the request made to the Association of Theological Schools (ATS) and the United States Department of Education (USDE) to transfer title IV gatekeeper status to ATS, including any relevant correspondence or notification from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office; (3) evidence of the approval of the transfer of title IV gatekeeper status to the Association of Theological Schools (ATS) by the United States Department of Education, including any relevant correspondence or notification from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office; and (4) evidence of notification to the institution's constituents related to the surrender of accreditation (Requirements of Affiliation 5 and 6, and Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). To further request that the institution complete and submit for approval, by June 1, 2020, a comprehensive, implementable teach-out plan (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education, upon voluntary surrender of accreditation, and include any teach-out agreements that the institution has entered into or intends to enter into with another institution. To note that accreditation will cease effective July 1, 2020.
