accred-hobart-and-william-smith-colleges.institution_unique_name: Hobart & William Smith Colleges | New York

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the supplemental information report directed by Commission action of October 14, 2022. To note that evidence of written communication to the institution's community regarding its decision to change accreditors was not received. To accept the institution's request to voluntarily surrender its accreditation and terminate its membership effective February 16, 2023. To note that a teach-out plan and teach-out agreements are not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the New York State Department of Education and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education. To note that the United States Department of Education Office of Federal Student Aid determined reasonable cause for the change in accreditor and issued approval for the change in accreditor on January 17, 2023, pursuant to DCL ID GEN 22-11 updated September 26, 2022, and DCL ID GEN 22-10 dated July 19, 2022. To note further that the Commission has determined the institutional change of accrediting agency is voluntary pursuant to 34 C.F.R. § 602.14(a). To note the New England Commission of Higher Education is the institutional accreditor for the institution as of February 16, 2023, and will now serve as the designated Title IV gatekeeper for the institution. To note that student records will be maintained at Hobart and William Smith Colleges, 337 Pulteney Street, Geneva, NY 14456. To note that MSCHE accreditation ceased on February 16, 2023, and Hobart and William Smith Colleges is no longer accredited by the Middle States Commission on Higher Education. To note this action is not retroactive.

The next evaluation visit is scheduled for 2023-2024.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation effective October 13, 2023. To note the complex substantive change request includes a transfer of 100% equity of Huertas College, Inc. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction by email to [email protected]. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due January 16, 2024, documenting evidence of: (1) status on the implementation of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all outstanding required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the Puerto Rico Board of Postsecondary Institutions and the United States Department of Education (Standard II); (3) updated financial statements (Standard VI); and (4) verification of the post-transaction governance structure (Standard VII). To direct a prompt Commission Liaison Guidance Visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus as soon as practicable but no later than six months after the anticipated date of the transaction October 13, 2023. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h). To note that the institution will begin self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2025-2026.

accred-ie-new-york-college--new-york--ms.institution_unique_name: IE New York College | New York

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective July 1, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note the complex substantive change request includes the inclusion of IE University (IEU) as sole member of Glasgow Caledonian New York College (GCNYC), effective July 1, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To note that written evidence of required approval has been provided by the New York State Education Department. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due December 2, 2024, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change including but not limited to evidence of the fully executed Collaboration and Support Agreement, Amended and Restated Bylaws, Member Substitution Agreement, and Charter Amendment, and the updated Master Services Agreement; (2) submission of any written and updated evidence of review, including but not limited to approvals from or a determination that the entity has declined to review the transaction, from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities including the United States Student Exchange and Visitor Program; (3) documented evidence of alternative funding sources for students previously receiving Title IV funding (Standard IV); (4) documented financial resources, funding base, and plans for financial development, including those from any related entities adequate to support its educational purposes and programs and to ensure sustained financial stability (Standard VI); (5) a clearly articulated and transparent governance structure that outlines roles, responsibilities, and accountability for inclusive decision making by each constituency, including the institution's legally constituted governing body, administration, faculty, staff, and students, as well as any related entities (Standard VII) (Fourteenth Edition and Evidence Expectations by Standard Guidelines); and (6) full disclosure of third-party providers including but not limited to clear definition of the terms of third-party arrangements and adequate and appropriate institutional review and approval of any student learning opportunities, student support services, assessment services, or outsourced operations designed, delivered, or assessed by third-party providers (Third-Party Providers Policy and Procedures). To note accreditation status cannot be acquired by, transferred, or extended to any third party (Related Entities Policy and Procedures). To note that approval from the United States Department of Education is no longer required because of the institution's intent to voluntarily relinquish Title IV eligibility effective if and only if the transaction occurs. To direct a complex substantive change site visit as soon as practicable but no later than six months after July 1, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations including post-transaction requirements. To note the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution and the related entity of their responsibility for disclosing, documenting, and explaining all significant matters and relationships involving related entities that may impact its compliance including but not limited to governance, independence and autonomy, management and administration, and financial viability and stability (Related Entities Policy and Procedures). To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h). To note that the institution will begin self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2026-2027.

To note the institution closed on August 31, 2023, while on adverse action status, without prior approval. To note the Commission has been unable to verify (1) implementation of the approved teach-out plan and teach-out agreements, including evidence that student records have been transferred to the final repository (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures) and (2) an orderly closure of the institution (Substantive Change Policy and Procedures and Requirement of Affiliation 2). To note that The King's College accreditation ceased effective August 31, 2023. As of September 1, The King's College is no longer accredited by the Middle States Commission on Higher Education.

accred-lackawanna-college--pennsylvania-.latest_action_label: Merger of Lackawanna College with Peirce College (effective June 30, 2025)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective June 30, 2025. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Lackawanna College with Peirce College, effective, June 30, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email [email protected]. To note that Lackawanna College is the surviving institution. To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the integrated institution. To note that Peirce College will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and effectuating the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note that Peirce College will cease accreditation effective July 30, 2026. To include 1608 Walnut Street, Philadelphia, PA 19106 as an additional location within the institution's scope of accreditation. To require immediate notification in the institution portal when instruction commences at the additional location. To note that the following credential levels are included within the scope of the institution's accreditation: post-baccalaureate certificate and master's degree or equivalent, integrating Peirce College graduate programs into Lackawanna College's accreditation status. To note that written documentation of review or approval from the following agency has been received: Pennsylvania Attorney General. To request a supplemental information report, due October 1, 2025, providing (1) updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including but not limited to: the United States Department of Education and the Pennsylvania Department of Education (Standard II); and (3) written evidence of the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus of Lackawanna College at 501 Vine St, Scranton, PA 18509 and to the additional location at 1608 Walnut Street, Philadelphia, PA 19106, as soon as practicable but no later than six months after June 30, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR 602.22(h). To note that the institution will begin self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2028-2029. To note the action for Peirce College on April 24, 2025.

In accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education, upon voluntary surrender of accreditation, and include any teach-out agreements that the institution has entered into or intends to enter into with another institution. To note that accreditation will cease effective July 1, 2020.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the teach-out plan. To approve the teach out plan. To approve the teach-out agreement with Bloomsburg University of Pennsylvania which will be renamed Commonwealth University of Pennsylvania on July 1, 2022. To note that Mansfield University of Pennsylvania's accreditation will cease as of the effective date of the integration, anticipated to be July 1, 2022. To note the transfer of Mansfield University of Pennsylvania student records, programs, and services to Commonwealth University of Pennsylvania. To note that Commonwealth University of Pennsylvania, Bloomsburg, PA has been designated as the repository of all Mansfield University of Pennsylvania student records. To require written notification when student records have been transferred to Commonwealth University of Pennsylvania. To note that degree granting authority from the Pennsylvania Department of Education will cease as of July 1, 2022. To note the action for Bloomsburg University of Pennsylvania on March 10, 2022.

accred-maryland-university-of-integrativ.institution_unique_name: Maryland University of Integrative Health | Maryland
accred-maryland-university-of-integrativ.latest_action_label: Merger of Maryland University of Integrative Health with Notre Dame of Maryland University (effective February 1, 2024)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective February 1, 2024. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Maryland University of Integrative Health with Notre Dame of Maryland University, effective, February 1, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction by email to [email protected] or, if there is a subsequent delay in the anticipated date of the closing of the transaction, to require immediate notification to the Commission of the revised anticipated transaction date. To note that Notre Dame of Maryland University is the surviving institution. To further note that Maryland University of Integrative Health's accreditation will cease effective January 31, 2025. To note Maryland University of Integrative Health will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and to effectuate the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due July 1, 2024, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change (Complex Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) update on the date Maryland University of Integrative Health's degree granting authority will cease (Requirement of Affiliation 1); and (3) written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and the Maryland Higher Education Commission (Standard II). To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the teach-out agreements. To approve the teach-out agreements with (1) Gannon University, Erie, PA; (2) Genesee Community College, Batavia, NY; (3) State University of New York College at Geneseo, Geneseo, NY; (4) LaRoche University, Pittsburgh, PA; (5) Mercy College, Dobbs Ferry, NY; (6) Roberts Wesleyan University, Rochester, NY; (7) State University of New York at Canton, Canton, NY; and (8) Erie Community College, Williamsville, NY. To postpone a decision and to request a supplemental information report, due September 6, 2023, providing (1) an update on the status of the institution and an update on the orderly closure of the institution (Requirement of Affiliation 2); (2) evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the New York State Education Department (NYSED) and the United States Department of Education (USDE) (Standard II); and (3) an update on the request to surrender degree granting authority from the New York State Education Department (NYSED). To note that degree granting authority from the New York State Department of Education will cease for Medaille University as of August 31, 2023. To note that Niagara University, Niagara University, NY has been designated as the final repository of all Medaille University student records, and that students may obtain academic records at Niagara University, 5795 Lewiston Road, Niagara University, NY 14109-2010. To note that the accreditation cease date for the institution and all of its locations has changed from October 31, 2023 to August 31, 2023.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the teach-out plan and teach-out agreements in response to the Commission action of May 22, 2023. To approve the teach-out agreements with: (1) Alfred State College, Alfred, NY; (2) Daemen University, Amherst, NY; (3) Canisius College, Buffalo, NY; (4) D'Youville University, Buffalo, NY; (5) Elmira College, Elmira, NY; (6) Hartwick College, Oneonta, NY; (7) Hilbert College, Hamburg, NY; (8) Houghton University, Houghton, NY; (9) Keuka College, Keuka Park, NY; (10) Nazareth College of Rochester, Rochester, NY; (11) Niagara University, Niagara University, NY; (12) Russell Sage College, Troy, NY; (13) Saint Vincent College and Seminary, Latrobe, PA; (14) State University of New York Brockport, Brockport, NY; (15) SUNY Buffalo State University, Buffalo, NY; (16) SUNY College of Technology at Delhi, Delhi, NY; (17) SUNY Fredonia, Fredonia, NY; (18) University of Pittsburgh, Pittsburgh, PA; (19) Utica University, Utica, NY; (20) Villa Maria College of Buffalo, Buffalo, NY; and (21) Wells College, Aurora, NY. To request an updated teach-out plan due August 1, 2023, to include: (1) evidence that communications regarding student records has occurred; (2) evidence that students have received information that is up-to-date, accurate, and included references to all teach-out options available to students; (3) evidence that financial aid counseling has been conducted; (4) evidence that onsite transfer events have taken place; (5) copies of any new signed teach-out agreements, including any documentation supporting the agreements; (6) evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the New York State Education Department and the United States Department of Education (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (7) evidence that Niagara University, Niagara University, NY has been designated as the final repository of all Medaille University student records, and that students may obtain academic records at Niagara University, 5795 Lewiston Road, Niagara University, NY 14109-2010. In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation, and include any signed, teach-out agreements that the institution has entered into or intends to enter into with another institution. To note the Commission will reject the teach-out plan and/or agreements and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the notification dated July 23, 2024, of the institution's intent to voluntarily surrender accreditation and terminate membership. Staff acted on behalf of the Commission to request a supplemental information report, due August 22, 2024, providing: (1) evidence that state or federal requirements and approvals regarding the surrender of the institution's accreditation status have been obtained or are in progress, and the institution must ascertain any state or federal requirements related to surrender or loss of its accreditation (Standard II); (2) information regarding any implications of the surrender on title IV/student financial aid program participation including any correspondence or notification from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office of the USDE, and USDE's approval of the transfer of gatekeeper status to another accreditor if applicable (Standard II); (3) copies of communications to the institution's community (students and other constituents) related to the surrender or loss of accreditation as appropriate (Standard II, Public Disclosures Policy, and Public Disclosure Procedures); and (4) an updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR).

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, or ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation. To note the complex substantive change request includes a merger of Columbia Central University with NUC University, effective December 1, 2022. To note that NUC University is the surviving institution. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction. To note that approval of the transaction by the Puerto Rico Board of Postsecondary Education has been received. To request a supplemental information report, due March 1, 2023, providing (1) the updated status of the merged institutions; (2) a final version of the merger milestones including timeframe and status (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures); (3) submission of any written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and the Florida Commission for Independent Education. To direct a complex substantive change site visit as soon as practicable but no later than six months after December 1, 2022, the anticipated date of the transaction. To note the transfer of Columbia Central University student records, programs, and services to NUC University. To note that the following additional locations are included within the institution's scope of accreditation: (1) Carretera 183 KM 1.7, Caguas, PR 00725; (2) Road 2 Km 15.2, 46C Street, Corujo Industrial Zone, Bayamon, PR 00961; (3) Victoria Industrial Park Building 1, Road 877, Km. 0.08, Carolina, PR 00987; (4) PO Box 3062 Calle Betances 3 Altos, Yauco, PR 00698; and (5) Rafael Cordero Avenue, Genova Street, Caguas Norte Urb, Caguas, PR 00726. To direct a substantive change site visit to all locations as soon as practicable but no later than six months after the commencement of instruction at each location. To require immediate notification when instruction commences at each location. To note that the Commission may rescind this action if instruction does not commence within one calendar year from the date of this action. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action. To note the action of Columbia Central University on November 17, 2022. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h) in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2025-2026.

accred-northeastern-seminary-at-roberts-.institution_name: Northeastern Seminary at Roberts Wesleyan College
accred-northeastern-seminary-at-roberts-.institution_unique_name: Northeastern Seminary at Roberts Wesleyan College | New York

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the supplemental information report directed by Commission action of December 18, 2023. To note that evidence of written communication to the institution's community regarding its decision to change accreditors was received. To accept the institution's request to voluntarily surrender its accreditation and terminate its membership effective April 30, 2024. To note that a teach-out plan and teach-out agreements are not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the New York State Department of Education and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education. To note The Association of Theological Schools (ATS) is the designated Title IV gatekeeper for the institution. To note that student records will be maintained at Northeastern Seminary at Roberts Wesleyan College, 2265 Westside Drive, Rochester, NY 14624. To note that MSCHE accreditation will cease on April 30, 2024, and as of May 1, 2024, Northeastern Seminary at Robert Wesleyan College, will no longer be accredited by the Middle States Commission on Higher Education.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the notification dated November 15, 2023, and received by the Commission on November 17, 2023, of the institution's intent to surrender accreditation status and terminate membership. To request a supplemental information report, due January 5, 2024, providing: (1) the anticipated date of the voluntary surrender (MARCHE Bylaws and Accreditation Actions Policy and Procedures); (2) state or federal requirements and written evidence of required approvals regarding the surrender of the institution's candidate for accreditation status and that the institution has ascertained and verified that any state or federal requirements related to surrender or loss of accreditation have been met (Standard II); (3) information regarding any implications of the surrender or loss of accreditation on Title IV/student financial aid program participation including any correspondence, notification, and/or written evidence of approvals from the Federal Student Financial Aid Office of the United States Department of Education (USDE), specifically verification that the Commission on Accrediting of the Association of Theological Schools is the designated gatekeeper for USDE (Standard II: Ethics and Integrity); and (4) copies of communications to the institution's community (students and other constituents) related to the surrender or loss of candidate for accreditation status as appropriate (Standard II, Standard VII, and Public Disclosures Policy and Procedures). To note that a teach-out plan is not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the New York State Education Department, and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education.
landing_actions.action_label_short: Voluntarily Surrendered Accreditation
landing_actions.notes: To acknowledge receipt of the notification dated November 15, 2023, and received by the Commission on November 17, 2023, of the institution's intent to surrender accreditation status and terminate membership. To note that a teach-out plan is not necessary because the institution will retain its degree granting authority from the New York State Education Department, and primary accreditation through another accreditor recognized by the United States Department of Education.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective January 3, 2024. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences with St. Joseph's University, effective, January 3, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction by email to [email protected] or, if there is a subsequent delay in the anticipated date of the closing of the transaction, to require immediate notification to the Commission of the revised anticipated transaction date. To note that Saint Joseph's University is the surviving institution. To further note that Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences accreditation will cease effective August 31, 2024. To note Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and to effectuate the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note that written approval from the following agency has been received: Office of Pennsylvania Attorney General, Orphan's Court. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due June 3 2024, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change; (Complex Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) update on the date Pennsylvania College of Health Science's degree granting authority will cease (Requirement of Affiliation 1); (3) written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education, the Pennsylvania Department of Education, and Pennsylvania State Board of Nursing (Standard II). To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action. To note the action for Saint Joseph's University on September 25, 2023.

To note that written approval from the following agency has been received: Office of Pennsylvania Attorney General, Orphan's Court. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due June 3 2024, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change; (Complex Substantive Change Policy and Procedures); (2) update on the date Pennsylvania College of Health Science's degree granting authority will cease (Requirement of Affiliation 1); (3) written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education, the Pennsylvania Department of Education, and Pennsylvania State Board of Nursing (Standard II). To remind the institution of its obligation to immediately inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission reserves the right to rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision and/or the requested substantive change is not implemented within one calendar year from the date of this action. To note the action for Saint Joseph's University on September 25, 2023.

accred-saint-joseph-s-university--pennsy.latest_action_label: Merger of Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences with Saint Joseph's University (effective January 3, 2024)

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective January 3, 2024. To note the complex substantive change request includes the merger of Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences with Saint Joseph's University, effective, January 3, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction by email to [email protected] or, if there is a subsequent delay in the anticipated date of the closing of the transaction, to require immediate notification to the Commission of the revised anticipated transaction date. To note that Saint Joseph's University is the surviving institution. To note that the surviving institution is responsible for ensuring the submission of all required reports and information on behalf of the integrated institution. To further note that Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences' accreditation will cease effective August 31, 2024. As of September 1, 2024, Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences will no longer be accredited. To note the Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences will maintain accreditation for the sole purpose of implementing the teach-out plan and agreements and effectuating the change in ownership process with the United States Department of Education per the United States Department of Education's Updated Guidance and Procedures for Changes in Ownership, dated September 15, 2022 (Electronic Announcement ID: GENERAL-22-70). To note that degree granting authority of Pennsylvania College of the Health Sciences will cease effective August 31, 2024. To note the transfer of Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences student records, programs, and services to Saint Joseph's University. To note the action for Pennsylvania College of Health Sciences on September 25, 2023. To note that written approval from the following agency has been received: Office of Pennsylvania Attorney General, Orphan's Court. To note that the following additional location is included within the institution's scope of accreditation: 850 Greenfield Road, Lancaster, PA 17601. To require immediate notification in the institution portal when instruction commences at the additional location. To note that the Commission may rescind this action if instruction does not commence within one calendar year from the date of this action. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due June 3, 2024, providing (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all outstanding required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education, the Pennsylvania Department of Education, and Pennsylvania State Board of Nursing (Standard II); and, (3) written evidence of the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location. To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations including post-transaction requirements. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus at 5600 City Avenue, Philadelphia, PA 19131 and the additional location at 850 Greenfield Road, Lancaster, PA 17601 as soon as practicable but no later than six months after the anticipated date of the transaction, January 1, 2024. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any development reveals additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision or the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h) in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2026-2027. To note the institution is currently in self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2023-2024.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation, effective September 8, 2025. To note that the complex substantive change request includes Strayer University's acquisition, through stock transfer, of the Media Design School (MDS). To note that MDS will become an international additional location of Strayer University in New Zealand. To include the Media Design School, 10 Madden Street, Auckland, 1010, New Zealand, as an additional location within the institution's scope of accreditation. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of the closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To require immediate notification by email to [email protected] if there are any material changes to the information provided in the substantive change request prior to the closing of the transaction. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due November 3, 2025, providing (1) an updated status of the implementation of the complex substantive change (Substantive Change Policy and Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review or approval of the transaction from all required licensing, regulatory, or legal entities, including but not limited to: the United States Department of Education, any applicable state licensing or authorization bodies in the United States, and any applicable New Zealand regulatory entities overseeing private training establishments, including the New Zealand Qualifications Authority (NZQA) (Standard II); (3) material updates to corporate governance or board structure, including but not limited to submission of an updated governance organizational chart and any applicable conflict-of-interest policies (Standard VII); (4) written evidence of the institution's updated Eligibility and Certification Approval Report (ECAR) reflecting the inclusion of the additional location; and (5) documentation of the design, delivery, and assessment of student learning experiences at the international additional location (Standard III and Standard V ). To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus at 1133 15th Street, North West, Washington, DC, 20005, as soon as practicable but no later than six months after September 8, 2025, the anticipated date of the transaction. To further direct that the complex substantive change site visit include the international additional location at Media Design School, 10 Madden Street, Auckland, 1010, New Zealand. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision or if the complex substantive change is not implemented within one year of this action. A new comprehensive visit is not required because the institution is currently in self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2025-2026.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the complex substantive change request. To include the change in legal status, form of control, and ownership within the institution's scope of accreditation effective December 5, 2024. To note the complex substantive change request includes an acquisition of Post University effective December 5, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To require notification of the date of the closing of the transaction within five calendar days of the transaction, or immediate notification if there is any delay of the date of closing of the transaction, by email to [email protected]. To request a complex substantive change supplemental information report, due March 3, 2025, documenting evidence of (1) the updated status of the complex substantive change including but not limited to evidence of any planned collaborative agreements with Post University (Complex Substantive Change Procedures); (2) written evidence of review, including but not limited to, approval of the transaction from all outstanding required licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the New England Commission of Higher Education (NECHE), Connecticut Office of Higher Education, and the United States Department of Education (Standard II); (3) updated financial statements (Standard VI); and (4) verification of the post-transaction governance structure (Standard VII). To direct a prompt Commission liaison guidance visit to discuss the Commission's expectations. To direct a complex substantive change site visit to the main campus as soon as practicable but no later than six months after December 5, 2024, the anticipated date of the transaction. To remind the institution of its obligation to inform the Commission about any and all developments relevant to this action. To note that Post University is not a related entity. To note the Commission may rescind this action if any developments reveal additional information that might have affected the Commission's decision. To direct the institution to conduct a new comprehensive evaluation in accordance with Commission policy and procedures and federal regulation 34 CFR § 602.22(h). To note that the institution is currently in self-study in anticipation of an evaluation visit in 2027-2028.

landing_actions.action_label: To acknowledge receipt of the teach-out plan and agreement. To approve the teach-out agreement with Saint Joseph's University. To request an updated teach-out plan, due on October 1, 2022 with (1) evidence that student records have been transferred to the final repository; (2) a final version of the integration planning team milestones including timeframe and status (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures); and (3) submission of any written evidence of approvals from all necessary licensing, regulatory, or other legal entities as necessary, including the United States Department of Education and the Pennsylvania Department of Education (Requirement of Affiliation 5). To note that University of the Sciences accreditation will cease on December 31, 2022. To note the transfer of University of the Sciences student records, programs, and services to Saint Joseph's University. To note that Saint Joseph's University, Philadelphia, PA has been designated as the repository of all University of the Sciences student records. To note that degree granting authority from the Pennsylvania Department of Education will cease as of December 31, 2022. To note the action for Saint Joseph's University on March 10, 2022.

accred-wells-college--new-york--msche.latest_action_label: To approve the following teach-out agreements: (1) Alfred University, Alfred, NY; (2) Daemen University, Amherst, NY; (3) Excelsior University, Albany, NY; (4) Hartwick College, Oneonta, NY; (5) Ithaca College, Ithaca, NY; (6) Keuka College, Keuka Park, NY; (7) Le Moyne College, Syracuse, NY; (8) Manhattanville University, Purchase, NY; (9) Mercy University, Dobbs Ferry, NY; (10) Niagara University, Niagara Falls, NY; (11) Russell Sage College, Troy, NY; (12) St.

landing_actions.action_label: To approve the following teach-out agreements: (1) Alfred University, Alfred, NY; (2) Daemen University, Amherst, NY; (3) Excelsior University, Albany, NY; (4) Hartwick College, Oneonta, NY; (5) Ithaca College, Ithaca, NY; (6) Keuka College, Keuka Park, NY; (7) Le Moyne College, Syracuse, NY; (8) Manhattanville University, Purchase, NY; (9) Mercy University, Dobbs Ferry, NY; (10) Niagara University, Niagara Falls, NY; (11) Russell Sage College, Troy, NY; (12) St. Thomas Aquinas College, Sparkhill, NY; (13) SUNY at Binghamton, Binghamton, NY; (14) State University of New York Brockport, Brockport, NY; (15) SUNY College at Geneseo, Geneseo, NY; (16) SUNY College at Potsdam, Potsdam, NY; (17) SUNY College of Agriculture and Technology at Morrisville, Morrisville, NY; (18) SUNY Cortland, Cortland, NY; (19) SUNY Oneonta, Oneonta, NY; and (20) University of Pittsburgh, Pittsburgh, PA. To postpone a decision on the following teach-out agreements pending confirmation that a determination has not been made by an independent auditor expressing doubt about the institution's ability to operate as a going concern, or an adverse opinion on a finding of material weakness, and evidence that the institution is not under a provisional program participation agreement with the United States Department of Education: (1) Albion College, Albion, MI; (2) Beloit College, Beloit, WI; (3) Hobart and William Smith Colleges, Geneva, NY; and (4) Roanoke College, Salem, VA. To note the institution will close by June 30, 2024, while on probation and without prior approval. To require an updated teach-out plan, due June 5, 2024, providing (1) documentation of financial and human resources necessary to implement the teach-out plan including but not limited to financial resources to meet staffing and payroll obligations; (2) evidence that required activities with the New York State Education Department and the United States Department of Education have been completed; (3) evidence that the institution met the request of the United States Department of Education on May 21, 2024, and all copies of documents provided to that agency; (4) compliance with state and federal laws, including but not limited to the Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act and with the New York State Attorney General; (5) information regarding any recruitment of online students for an anticipated bachelor of arts in theater degree; (6) documentation of appropriate advisement and support services specific to the needs of international students in the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP); (7) documentation and appropriate advisement and support services specific to the needs of student athletes and reporting to the National Collegiate Athletics Association (NCAA); (8) evidence that communications regarding student records has occurred; and (9) evidence of a finalized agreement and the arrangements for the permanent disposition of student records so that students and alumni will be able to obtain accurate and complete transcripts now and in the future, and (10) evidence of a finalized agreement for institutional records and the process and date by which records have been transferred to the final repository (Teach-Out Plans and Agreements Policy and Procedures). In accordance with Commission policy and federal regulations, the teach-out plan must provide for the equitable treatment of students to complete their education or transfer to another institution, if the Commission were to withdraw accreditation. To note the Commission may reject the teach-out plan and require resubmission if all of the required information is not provided. To remind the institution and teach-out partners that exclusivity agreements and legacy arrangements are prohibited. To note that the date accreditation will cease will be determined following the submission of the updated teach-out plan, due June 5, 2024.
